title: Portugal World Cup 2026 Preview: Lineup, Best Players, Group, & Picks
original_text:
Portugal is headed to World Cup 2026, set to compete in Group K, and has several notable storylines surrounding the squad. Portuguese legend Cristiano Ronaldo will feature in his sixth — and potentially final — World Cup, still seeking one of the few trophies he’s missing in his overcrowded collection.

The Seleção das Quinas is expected by many to be one of the last teams standing to lift the trophy when World Cup 2026 is all said and done.

Below, we’ll break down Portugal’s projected starting lineup, best players, group outlook, strengths and weaknesses, and top World Cup picks on PrizePicks.

Over on PrizePicks Team Picks , you can make predictions on how national teams will perform in the 2026 World Cup , including tournament winner, group winner, and knockout-stage advancement markets.

Portugal enters World Cup 2026 boasting arguably the strongest midfield trio in the competition, with each player playing a vital role on the team. Expect Bruno Fernandes to serve as an attacking midfielder, aiding an already strong Portuguese attack.

PSG stars Vitinha and Joao Neves should play further behind Fernandes, each serving as a deep-lying playmaker who moves freely through central midfield.

The Portugal World Cup XI could change based on form, injuries, and matchups, but Portugal’s core will be built around a strong attack with a strong midfield and two wing-backs who will be key to what the team does on both sides of the ball.

Despite his age, Ronaldo is set to remain the primary striker, though some argue it may be time for him to be a substitute.

Portugal qualified for the 2026 World Cup after topping Group F in UEFA qualifiers.

The Seleção das Quinas won four of six matches, outscoring the group 20-7 in those matches.

Fernandes is set to feature in his third World Cup after a record-breaking season at Manchester United. The 31-year-old now holds the English Premier League single-season assist record, surpassing EPL legends Thierry Henry and Kevin de Bruyne, who each had 20 in a season.

The Portuguese star will now look to bring his playmaking and goal-scoring to the biggest international stage with his country seeking its first World Cup title.

Fernandes led Portugal with 5 G/A in the 2022 World Cup and returns a 3.33x payout (via PrizePicks) to lead the Seleção das Quinas in goals scored this summer.

Fernandes is also among the favorites to receive the Golden Ball trophy for the tournament with a 14.28x payout on PrizePicks.

Portugal could be one of the more fun teams to watch, owning a solid mixture of individual stars and fluid formations that help to break down defenses.

Roberto Martinez’s squad enters the competition in strong form, unbeaten in its last five matches and having won its final three straight pre-tournament friendlies ahead of the opener against DR Congo.

While still anticipated to top the group, Portugal may find some of its group stage matches a bit cagey based on what we’ve seen early on at the 2026 World Cup.

Here are some Portugal picks on PrizePicks for the 2026 World Cup. Check out more of our soccer picks and predictions on Playbook.

The World Cup payouts listed below indicate real-money earnings if a prediction is correct; for example, a 2x on a $100 entry pays out $200 on PrizePicks Team Picks . Payouts are live as of writing but are subject to change.

As previously mentioned, Portugal is certainly considered a favorite to win its first World Cup in what could be a legendary send-off for one of the greatest players of all time.

Martinez’s side enters the World Cup following a UEFA Nations League title just last summer, taking down some of Europe’s top teams, which they may face once again this summer.

Portugal is the early favorite to top Group K , and with good reason.

They share the stage with World Cup debutants Uzbekistan, a DR Congo side that has not played in the World Cup since 1974, and a Colombian side that failed to qualify for the competition in 2022 but is considered a possible threat to Portugal in Group K.

Based on FIFA’s new format and the implications of this PrizePicks payout, it seems difficult to imagine a world without Portugal advancing to the knockout stage of the World Cup.

Portugal has its eyes on the prize in what seems to be Ronaldo’s final World Cup appearance. The Seleção das Quinas hope to finally get over the hump and win its second major international trophy in the last two years.
